short_description:
ACELAB is a technology company based in New York, founded in 2019 or 2020 by a team of architects, including CEO Vardhan Mehta. The company focuses on improving the architecture and construction industry through its AI-powered platform, Materials Hub. This platform addresses inefficiencies in the building materials market by offering data-driven tools for architects, designers, and building professionals.

Materials Hub enables users to discover and compare building materials effectively. It interprets natural language queries to suggest suitable materials from a comprehensive database of over 100,000 products. The platform also facilitates collaboration among teams and integrates with design software like Revit, streamlining workflows. With over 20,000 architecture and design firms using Materials Hub, including many of the top firms in the U.S., ACELAB is committed to enhancing material selection and specification processes for better project outcomes.

The company provides cloud-based tools designed to assess, optimize, and manage building operations through data-driven insights. AkitaBox manages over 500 million square feet across various sectors, including education, healthcare, government, and commercial real estate.

AkitaBox serves a diverse range of clients, including educational institutions and government entities, helping them streamline operations and improve efficiency.

The flagship product, BQE CORE, is a comprehensive platform that centralizes time tracking, project management, accounting, and billing. BQE CORE is designed for A&E firms and has gained over 3,000 users, including notable clients like Eagle Engineering and Circular. The company emphasizes continuous improvement and customer feedback, ensuring that its solutions evolve to meet the needs of its users.

Integrated Environmental Solutions (IES) provides Virtual Environment (IESVE) software for integrated performance-based building analysis and digital twin tools.
short_description:
IES Ltd. is a global climate tech company based in Glasgow, United Kingdom, specializing in software solutions and consultancy services aimed at decarbonizing the built environment. Founded in 1994, IES has become a leader in integrated performance-based analysis for buildings, employing approximately 528 people and generating annual revenue of $71.1 million. The company has significantly contributed to the performance improvement of over 1.5 million buildings worldwide.

The flagship product, IESVE (IES Virtual Environment), is an energy analysis and performance modeling software suite that offers tools for energy analysis, HVAC system design, daylighting analysis, and more. IES also provides digital twin solutions and real-time performance monitoring through its ICL product suite and IES Live. input_description: An AI-driven platform focusing on enhancing design workflows and decision-making processes for architects and engineers.
short_description:
Vavetek AI is a Singapore-based SaaS startup founded in 2023, specializing in AI and machine learning solutions for the Architecture, Engineering, and Construction (AEC) industry. The company focuses on optimizing large-scale construction projects by reducing costs, automating processes, and enhancing efficiency. Vavetek AI's headquarters is located at High Street Plaza in Singapore.

The company's flagship product, BAMROC, is an AI-powered copilot designed to automatically detect and resolve clashes in Building Information Modeling (BIM) models, particularly for Mechanical, Electrical, and Plumbing (MEP) systems. BAMROC can resolve up to 94% of clashes in large projects and offers features like rebar congestion detection and 3D modeling automation. Vavetek AI also provides a range of services that include BIM coordination, energy optimization, and workflow automation, all aimed at improving project efficiency and sustainability.

input_description: A skills management and workforce planning platform that helps firms visualize their employees' capabilities for better project staffing.
short_description:
Visual Workforce is a software development company based in Richmond, Virginia, founded in 2017. With a small team, the company specializes in skills management, talent intelligence, and workforce planning platforms. It evolved from an internal tool of an IT staffing and recruiting firm into a comprehensive skills intelligence platform that helps organizations make data-driven decisions about their talent.

The core offering is a talent management solution that includes skills tracking, an AI-powered skills engine, and workforce visualizations. These features allow organizations to capture employee skills, identify gaps, and support strategic planning. The platform emphasizes user-friendly visualizations, enabling rapid insights for professional services and IT sectors. Visual Workforce aims to enhance internal mobility, succession planning, and team building while optimizing talent management processes.

input_description: Specializes in customized software solutions and technology consulting to improve project delivery and operational efficiency in AEC.
short_description:
WPM Technology is a full-service IT firm based in Houston, Texas, and a division of Walter P Moore, established in 2017. The company specializes in providing technology solutions tailored for the Architecture, Engineering, and Construction (AEC) industry. With a team of 11-50 employees, WPM Technology leverages over 25 years of IT experience to serve as a trusted advisor for AEC firms and related markets.

The company offers a range of services, including IT managed services, IT consulting and advisory, cybersecurity measures, custom software development, and advanced visualization tools. WPM Technology focuses on delivering reliable and resilient technology solutions that align with business growth objectives. Their expertise in navigating IT complexities and preparing organizations for cyber incidents positions them as a dedicated partner in the AEC sector.
